programme.title: Escaping Polygamy: The Cost of Temptation
programme.desc: Two of Jessica and Shanell's siblings are being lured back to The Order by their father, Daniel.

programme.title: Escaping Polygamy: The Insider

programme.title: Escaping Polygamy: The Prophet's Son
programme.desc: Jessica meets with Raymond Jeffs, son of Warren Jeffs, the notorious leader and prophet of the FLDS polygamist cult. Raymond pleads for their help getting his only brother out of the group.

programme.title: Escaping Polygamy: Family Under Fire
programme.desc: Jessica and Andrea receive word from Jesse, a father desperate for his family to escape the polygamous group known as the AUB.

programme.title: Intervention: Dallas
programme.desc: After her mother attempted suicide, Dallas turned to heroin to deal with the confusion and pain and now lives on the streets.

programme.title: Intervention: Joe
programme.desc: At age eight, Joe learned the 'choking game' and choked himself daily throughout childhood. In high school, Joe discovered heroin and has become an addict. His family is desperate to help him.

programme.title: Intervention: Jimbo
programme.desc: Jimbo spends his days snorting drugs and flying into rages that terrorize both his family and community.

programme.title: Intervention: Nathan
programme.desc: An outstanding athlete, Nathan excelled at sports at an early age, but when he was 19, a fight at a local bar left him with a ruptured ACL, a painful injury that left him with an addiction to prescription opioids.

programme.title: Intervention: Courtney
programme.desc: An adventurous child with a love for animals, Courtney dreamed of growing up to be a veterinarian. Tragically, when she was just seven years old, her best friend died in a drowning accident and Courtney was never the same.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: The Parent Trap
programme.desc: The guys get stuck in a grocery post-it showdown, and tonight's big loser earns a time-out for his outrageous behavior at a kids' play space.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: The Butt of the Joker
programme.desc: The guys pair up in focus group presentations, and tonight's loser tries to cure the public of a nasty habit, but gets people smoking mad.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: No Child Left Behind
programme.desc: The guys pitch toys that should be kept away from tots, face off at a market, and see sports at the mall. The big loser sticks his nose where it doesn't belong.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Speech Impediment
programme.desc: Joe, Q, Sal and Murr crack each other up from behind glass, then test out awkward proposals. Plus, the big loser gets tongue-tied while speaking to government officials, and later, Joey Fatone goes behind the scenes on after party.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Card Against Humanity
programme.desc: Q, Murr, Joe and Sal have customers seeing red while working at an optician's office, then get too close for comfort eavesdropping on strangers' conversations. Plus, the big loser gives the business at a networking event.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: The Running of the Bullies
programme.desc: Q, Murr, Joe and Sal offer up peculiar specials at a steakhouse and get creepy while helping to clean a prop house. Plus, tonight's big loser realizes he may need his own lifeguard after making waves at the pool.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Washed Up
programme.desc: Q, Joe, Sal and Murr rat each other out at a pizza place and get into textual relationships with strangers at the mall. Then, the big loser twerks hard for the money at an impromptu bikini car wash.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: To Hatch a Predator
programme.desc: The Jokers perform unusual methods of DNA testing and get too close for comfort at the grocery store. Then, tonight's big loser becomes a shell of a man after he cracks under pressure during a presentation at a children's museum.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Crash Test Dummies
programme.desc: Murr, Sal, Joe and Q take some customers for a wild ride, and things get messy when they ask fellow shoppers for a hand. Plus, the big losers suffer a monster punishment that leaves them crushed.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Full Mental Jacket
programme.desc: Joe, Murr, Q and Sal send blood pressure rates sky-high while posing as nurses, then stroll through the store with their wives. Plus, the big loser wishes he could check out early at a pop-up fashion show.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Blue Man Dupe
programme.desc: Murr, Joe, Q and Sal are a little too generous with fellow shoppers at the market, then ask strangers to help them out of a texting jam. Plus, the big loser fights his way, repeatedly, to the best seat in the house.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Off the Reservation
programme.desc: Murr, Q, Joe and Sal participate in a fake documentary, then find themselves in the middle of a marshmallow throwing contest. Plus, tonight's big loser is the host with the least when he can't find customers' dinner reservations.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Well...
programme.desc: Q, Sal, Joe and Murr play a game of tag at a coffee shop, then convince shoppers to deliver sketchy messages at the grocery store. Plus, tonight's big loser might lose his mind in Impractical Jokers' first-ever non-punishment punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Sun-Fan Lotion
programme.desc: Murr, Q and Sal are at the massage parlor for much kneaded relaxation, and then ask strangers their opinions on questionable yearbook ads. Later, tonight's big loser has an awkward but fan-tastic time in the sun.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Urine Trouble
programme.desc: The guys play receptionists looking for suspicious names. The guys seek marriage advice from strangers after a private eye shares incriminating photos. The big loser goes from pissed off to pissed on in the matter of one punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: The Paternity Test
programme.desc: Joe, Sal, Q and Murr pose as concierges in a hotel lobby, and then commit insurance fraud with the help of some confused strangers. Afterwards, tonight's loser suffers a personal punishment that's anything but child's play.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Dinner Party Show 4
programme.desc: You're cordially invited to a one-of-a-kind dinner party at Joe, Q, Murr, and Sal's homes from the comfort of your own.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Dinner Party Show 5
programme.desc: The guys dine and discuss their week. Find out who can make the best meal and how long Q's beard has become.

programme.title: Adam Ruins Everything: Adam Ruins a Plate of Nachos
programme.desc: Adam dishes out the facts on the avocado trade being ruled by drug cartels, and big pork making bacon a pop culture hit while covering up its huge health risks. Plus, America's food problems stem from corn.

programme.title: Adam Ruins Everything: Adam Ruins a Sitcom
programme.desc: Adam tackles classic television stereotypes, from the racism behind public pools, to the 'model minority' myth of Asian Americans, to the on-screen toxic masculinity that's masking the problems young men face today.

programme.title: Adam Ruins Everything: Adam Ruins Doing Good
programme.desc: Adam takes down the do-gooders and reveals how 'Teach for America' is harmful to both students and teachers. He then uncovers the ineffectiveness of recycling and calls out the billionaires who create charities for tax breaks.

programme.title: Adam Ruins Everything: Adam Ruins Music
programme.desc: It's time to face the music, as Adam reveals the racism behind rock & roll's origins and the egregious fees of Live Nation's monopoly. He then defends musicians who 'sell out' to make a living in today's digital landscape.

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians: Bun in the Oven

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians: Diamonds Are Forever

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians: A Tangled Web

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians: Kris Jenner's Legacy
programme.desc: When Scott realizes that the girls know very little about Kris's history, he sets out to make her a commemorative legacy video. Meanwhile Kendall's anxiety strikes again right before Milan Fashion week.

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians: The Gender Reveal

programme.title: Lone Star Law: Wildcat Garage
programme.desc: Deer season is well underway across Texas. In East Texas, Game Wardens find a bobcat a new home and investigate an illegally killed deer. On the coast, the Texas Game Wardens deal with an outstanding warrant.

programme.title: Lone Star Law: Bucks Collide
programme.desc: Game wardens head to a home after getting a call about bucks fighting in Comal County. While patrolling the Brazos River, Kiel responds to a drowning in progress. A trespassing incident quickly turns into a drug case. Putnam hears shots fired nearby.

programme.title: Lone Star Law: Anglers, Alcohol and Owls
programme.desc: In Texas, a woman is found in distress on the side of the highway. Warden Hensz handles an aggressive family of owls, while Warden Bailey responds to a call about underage drinkers holding a fishing tournament.

programme.title: Lone Star Law: Caught By Surprise
programme.desc: Warden Price is on a mission to arrest out-of-state hunters with warrants for trespassing and hunting on private property. A young angler is haunted by his past and is taken to county jail, and Warden Richards questions a pair of dishonest fishermen.

programme.title: Lone Star Law: Tempers Are Rising
programme.desc: Warden Cummings deals with an angry man who blew a stop sign. Warden Boone helps chase a trio of armed robbers on the run, and Warden Inman investigates a group of suspected deer hunters for trespassing.

programme.title: Lone Star Law: Trashed
programme.desc: Across Texas, game wardens work day and night to enforce the law. In East Texas, a young woman is served an arrest warrant. A fisherman has no fishing license but plenty of outstanding warrants. Outside Austin, someone illegally dumps a pile of trash.

programme.title: Lone Star Law: Bullets and Lies
programme.desc: A couple is suspected of killing a deer during off-season. Warden Iles transports orphaned baby armadillos for rehabilitation, and Warden Gould searches for a mountain lion that is lurking near a recreational park in Laredo.

programme.title: Lone Star Law: Tempers Are Rising
programme.desc: Warden Cummings deals with an angry man who blew a stop sign. Warden Boone helps chase a trio of armed robbers on the run, and Warden Inman investigates a group of suspected deer hunters for trespassing.

programme.title: Lone Star Law: Caught By Surprise
programme.desc: Warden Price is on a mission to arrest out-of-state hunters with warrants for trespassing and hunting on private property. A young angler is haunted by his past and is taken to county jail, and Warden Richards questions a pair of dishonest fishermen.

programme.title: Lone Star Law: Bucks and Bows
programme.desc: A hunter is confronted for killing a deer with a gun in bowhunting only season. A man is questioned for being in possession of venomous copperhead snakes. While checking bowhunters at night, a game warden helps find an injured hunter.

programme.title: Lone Star Law: Gator Roundup
programme.desc: On this special episode, Texas game wardens round up alligators large and small, from a 14-foot gator in a neighbourhood lake to a small blind gator at risk for poaching.

programme.title: Lone Star Law: Out for Blood
programme.desc: It's the start of deer season, and the Texas woods are filled with hunters trying to bag a prize buck. Game Wardens are busy checking hunters across the state, while also investigating a rash of deer stand thefts, and rescuing a trio of baby hawks.

programme.title: Lone Star Law: To Catch a Poacher
programme.desc: A team of Wardens deploy on a multi-county night operation to stop spotlighters and road hunters. Texas Game Wardens check teal hunters on opening weekend. A dead deer found with an arrow still in it leads Game Wardens to a poacher.

programme.title: Yukon Men: Dark Days
programme.desc: The Yukon Men are enduring through what is known as the dark days. Residents team up to keep a large wolf pack at bay. The Moore family must choose a leader for their dog-mushing team. Joey honours his grandmother by hosting a traditional dinner for her.

programme.title: Yukon Men: The Black Wolf
programme.desc: A wolf pack continues to encroach. Charlie's only option is to pursue the pack leader. Joey deals with consequences of Alaska's unusually warm winter and turns to his dad for help. Nations thinks the Moore's mushing team is running with the wrong leader.

programme.title: Yukon Men: On Thin Ice
programme.desc: Stan has the dangerous task of building an ice road across the Yukon River. Joe and Bob take their chances to embark on a perilous caribou hunt. Charlie confronts Nations about his trapline neglect. Pat and Courtney are faced with an alarming discovery.

programme.title: Yukon Men: Gut Check
programme.desc: The pressure is on Nations when the Moores head out for the first major dog sled race of the season. Stan faces a hard decision when one of his dogs falls ill. Charlie has had enough once he sees the state of the trapline he handed over to Nations.

programme.title: Yukon Men: Tanana's Test
programme.desc: Stan and Pat race to prepare Tanana to be a checkpoint for the Iditarod dog sled race. Charlie and Bob hunt a grizzly that has been terrorizing a miner's camp. Joe trains his new dog, hoping to turn him into a hunting partner.

programme.title: Yukon Men: Life on the Line
programme.desc: Nations and Charlie have a confrontation over rights to a family trapline. Stan and Kathleen try to upgrade Tozi cabin, but get more than they bargained for. The Moore's kennel is on the line as Courtney and Nations race in the Yukon River Championship.

programme.title: Yukon Men: Blood on Their Hands
programme.desc: After a poor trapping season, Charlie Wright teams up with Stan Zuray in a last-ditch effort to catch beaver. Stan and Joe Zuray discover a large predator has been stealing from their trapline. James Roberts teaches his two sons the importance of hunting.

programme.title: Yukon Men: All In
programme.desc: Unseasonably thin ice prevents villagers from reaching valuable hunting grounds, forcing Stan and Charlie to carve a new path through the rugged Alaskan wilderness in search of food. Desperate to provide for his family, James takes a huge risk.

programme.title: Yukon Men: Breaking Point
programme.desc: The Yukon River breaks up earlier than ever. Despite dangerous conditions, Joey crosses the river to hunt geese. Stan and Bob prepare the village for imminent flooding due to the break up, when they realize someone is stuck.

programme.title: Yukon Men: Tanana's Last Stand
programme.desc: The largest wildfire in Alaskan history torches the land surrounding Tanana and threatens to devour the village. With state resources spread thin, the villagers must band together to battle the blaze or risk losing everything.

programme.title: Yukon Men: From the Ashes
programme.desc: After Alaska's largest wildfire ravages Tanana, the villagers must adapt their subsistence lifestyles to a new, even harsher reality. Two young homesteaders embark upon a dangerous journey in the hopes of starting a new life on the Cosna.

programme.title: Movie: Tyler Perry's Acrimony

programme.desc: A well-traveled serviceman and his wife settle down after finding a job in small town Mississippi. Ben and Erin have been given an all-in budget and are tasked with satisfying the couple's wish list that includes a historic home with a chef's kitchen.

programme.title: Home Town: Inspired By the Sea
programme.desc: A woman who has been renting for years is ready to buy a home, and her wish list includes a master bathroom with a big bathtub. Ben and Erin capitalize on the charm of the house and design a Nantucket-inspired kitchen and a stunning master suite.

programme.title: Home Town: Countryside in the City
programme.desc: A couple is ready for a different pace after living in the country for years. They're hoping to find a historic home that lets them hold onto the countryside with a spacious garden. Ben and Erin show them two very different options.

programme.title: Home Town: The Littlest House
programme.desc: A business owner that's been living with her mom is ready to find a place she can call her own. With an all-in budget of $100,000, she's looking for a small home with a porch. Ben and Erin transform the house using breathtaking Americana charm.

programme.title: Home Town: Southern Italianate Charm
programme.desc: Ben and Erin Napier get the opportunity to find and restore a home for some long-time friends. They're hoping to find a historical house with a porch and cozy charm.

programme.title: Home Town: The Sky's the Limit
programme.desc: A man who has always moved around and loves to travel and meet new people is looking for a large home. Ben and Erin can't believe their luck when he gives them a limitless budget to design a gorgeous retreat with a Spanish-style kitchen and master suite.

programme.title: Home Town: Small Town Life Awaits
programme.desc: A Canadian couple looks to purchase a vacation home that will one day become their retirement home. With an all-in budget of $140,000, Ben and Erin show them two Craftsman cottages.

programme.title: Love It or List It: All About That Basement

programme.title: Below Deck: Mediterranean: Walking on Broken Glass
programme.desc: The love triangle intensifies as Joao continues to send mixed messages to both Brooke and Kasey, which prompts them to confront him. A night out at a Michelin Starred restaurant inspires Adam to step up his culinary game for the rest of the season.

programme.title: Below Deck: Mediterranean: Bizarre Love Triangle
programme.desc: While the crew works hard to pull off a magical picnic for the guests on the island of Capri, Joao earns the praise of Captain Sandy for putting out all the water toys as the solo deckie. Later, the love triangle appears to be tipping in Kasey's favour.

programme.title: Below Deck: Mediterranean: Panic at the Deck-o
programme.desc: Joao solidifies the love triangle, leaving Kasey confused. On the crew day off, Hannah feels slighted by Brooke when she pursues a relationship with Joao. Meanwhile, Adam gets ready for his chance at redemption as he goes foraging for food.

programme.title: Pawn Stars: Getting a Head
programme.desc: The Pawn Stars are presented with a 1969 Buick Skylark. Chumlee and the Old Man look at a Civil War field desk with captured Confederate documents. Rick is shown a human shrunken head.

programme.title: Pawn Stars: Chummobile
programme.desc: The Pawn Stars have an opportunity to buy one of the most recognizable cars of all time: The Batmobile. A letter signed by Thomas Jefferson is found.

programme.title: Pawn Stars: Over the Moon
programme.desc: The Pawn Stars prepare for blast off when a piece of heat shield from Apollo 11 rockets into the shop.

programme.title: Pawn Stars: Take the Money and Run
programme.desc: Take aim with the Pawn Stars when the guys check out a Kentucky Long Rifle.

programme.title: Pawn Stars: Beam Me Up
programme.desc: The guys check out a postcard signed by boxer Jack Johnson and some props from the original Star Trek series.

programme.title: Pawn Stars: Dog Day Afternoon
programme.desc: It's dog-eat-dog for the Pawn Stars when Corey's pet pooch spends a day at the shop. Is it man's best friend or the Old Man's worst nightmare? Then, a guy brings in a cavalry sword used by John Wayne in the movie "Rio Grande."

programme.title: Pawn Stars: You Say You Wanna Revolution
programme.desc: The guys check out an Annual Register from 1776. Corey checks out a set of gambling tokens from the Nevada State Prison. Rick sentences Chumlee to a week of hard labour.

programme.title: Pawn Stars: I'll Be Doggone
programme.desc: Rick checks out a jetpack from the Gemini space program. Then, Chumlee's nuts over a Snoopy comic book with a personal drawing from Charles Schulz.

programme.title: Pawn Stars: Rescue 9-1-Chum
programme.desc: Rick and the Old Man check out a vintage smoke suit air pump. Corey looks to cash in on a silver certificate when he finds an unexpected error. Later, Chumlee gets hosed when he shows up to work late again.

programme.title: Pawn Stars: Chords, Swords and Rewards
programme.desc: Rick and Corey check out a sword belonging to the Freemasons. Will the guys be able to sneak in an offer or will negotiations get chopped? Then, Corey and Chumlee play with the idea of scoring a Gibson Master Museum guitar.

programme.title: Pawn Stars: Purple Haze
programme.desc: A collection of Viking items are brought into the shop. Then, Rick checks out a Fender Stratocaster played in the studio by Jimi Hendrix.

programme.title: Pawn Stars: Field Trip
programme.desc: The Old Man and Chumlee discover signed photos of Enola Gay; Semi Moseley's personal guitar jams into the shop.

programme.title: Pawn Stars: Mail Mayhem
programme.desc: A signed first edition of 'The Wizard of Oz' and a one of a kind Turbo Drive II arcade game arrive at the shop. Also, Rick goes searching for a package that was supposedly delivered to him.

programme.title: Pawn Stars: Hidden Pawn
programme.desc: A 1769 playing card with a political message; a motorcycle that was owned by actor Dennis Hopper; an antique stained glass lamp; three 'Charlie's Angels' figurines sneak in.

programme.title: Pawn Stars: A Pawn of Lies
programme.desc: Two original lithographs by graphic artist M.C. Escher; a censored recording made on a Soviet X-ray; a Wells Fargo model stagecoach; a 1950s toy lie detector.

programme.title: Pawn Stars: Pawnball Wizard
programme.desc: Aim for the high score with the Pawn Stars when a rare Captain Fantastic pinball machine comes into the shop.

programme.title: Pawn Stars: The Pawnshine State
programme.desc: Rick and Chumlee travel to the Sunshine State to look at a fully loaded 1954 Rybovich Yacht. Rick visits another pawn shop while in Florida, owned by his friend Lisa. Meanwhile in Vegas, Corey's quite busy with a huge Incredible Hulk comic collection.

programme.title: Pawn Stars: Business Is Brewing

programme.title: Pawn Stars: King of Pawntus

programme.title: Pawn Stars: Big Fish in a Small Pawn
programme.desc: Rick tackles a set of San Francisco 49ers Super Bowl rings with a high asking price. Then, Rick and Corey check out a spear gun.

programme.title: Movie: Sweet Bird of Youth

programme.title: Movie: The Black Stallion
programme.desc: A boy's adventure with a magnificent black stallion results in a close friendship.

programme.title: Jessie: Rossed at Sea Part Three

programme.title: Movie: Ratatouille

programme.title: Bunk'd: We Didn't Start the Fire

programme.title: Bunk'd: Take the Cake

programme.desc: The kids explore the booths at Camp Kikiwaka’s Olde Moose Rump Days. They find an old wooden box from Zuri’s booth and suddenly find themselves in the 1600s.

programme.desc: Jessie, Bertram and the kids head to Bali for a family vacation, but when their private jet starts to fail, Jessie takes control of the cockpit and makes an emergency landing on a tropical island.

programme.desc: After finding a nest of eggs on the terrace, Jessie and Ravi discover that Mr. Kipling is really Ms. Kipling, and a mom-to-be. After misplacing her eggs, Ravi realizes he may have to work at becoming a better grandparent to baby lizards.

programme.title: NCIS: Loose Cannons
programme.desc: Dr. Cyril Taft experiences the rush of an NCIS case when he is asked to perform surgery on Gibbs' suspect and finds key evidence. Also, Dinozzo pays Jeanne Benoit a visit as he chases a lead involving doctors in Sudan.

programme.desc: The NCIS agents' personal plans are interrupted when they each catch errors in a seemingly cut-and-dry case. Also, McGee and Delilah argue over the importance of not discussing work during dinner, while DiNozzo bores his date with shop talk.

programme.title: Strange Evidence: Stargate in the Jungle

programme.title: Strange Evidence: Vampire Down Under
programme.desc: A strange creature on the site of one of the worst nuclear disasters ever sparks fears of a terrifying genetic mutation. New evidence suggests a legendary predator with a thirst for blood might have come back from the dead.

programme.title: Secrets of the Underground: Doomsday Volcano Mystery

programme.title: Secrets of the Underground: King Solomon's Treasures

programme.title: Secrets of the Underground: Mass Grave Under Pennsylvania
programme.desc: Rob hunts for a legendary mass grave that could expose a dark secret of America's industrial past. High in the mountains, a giant lake is mysteriously disappearing, and Rob uses cutting-edge technology to find out why.

programme.title: Secrets of the Underground: Texan Mass Extinction
programme.desc: Rob Nelson descends into a cave 30 feet beneath Texas in search of evidence of a mass extinction event. Then, he heads to Lake Michigan to investigate an unexplained, Bermuda Triangle-like phenomenon that has caused ships to disappear for centuries.

programme.title: Mythical Beasts: Blood for the Snake God

programme.title: Mythical Beasts: Curse of the Vampire
programme.desc: Terrifying new discoveries reveal the bloody truth behind the legends of vampires, and experts investigate why people believed these monsters had a taste for human blood.

programme.title: Mythical Beasts: Hunting the Loch Ness Monster

programme.title: Mythical Beasts: The Dragon's Inferno
programme.desc: New discoveries reveal hard evidence showing that dragons were not just the stuff of nightmares, and experts use the latest science to explain why people believed in these wicked creatures.

programme.title: Mythical Beasts: Demons of the Devil
programme.desc: The latest science reveals that demons aren't just the stuff of horror stories, and as experts investigate shocking new archaeological evidence, they uncover the gruesome methods used to exorcise humans of demonic possessions.

programme.title: Mythical Beasts: The Kraken Awakens
programme.desc: New discoveries suggest that a sea beast like the Kraken could have existed, and experts examine a strange monster that could give shocking answers into this underwater mystery.

programme.title: Mythical Beasts: Call of the Werewolf
programme.desc: New evidence reveals why a powerful hallucinogenic drug could be to blame for the werewolf, and people suspected of the power were hunted and killed, but even so, many admitted that they could become these deadly creatures.

programme.desc: A Connecticut couple's first flip spirals out of control as they go further over budget with every project they tackle. While she struggles to get things back on track, his love of new tools and expensive fixtures constantly interferes.

programme.desc: Paid programming.

programme.title: Mega Decks: The Ultimate Luxury Deck with a Two-Story Waterfall
programme.desc: A couple challenges the crew to build a deck that maximizes their gorgeous views, and adds 1000 square feet of outdoor living space.

programme.title: Mega Decks: A Log Home Gets a Two-Story Cliffside Deck
programme.desc: Mark loves his cliffside log home in the Rocky Mountains, but the small existing deck doesn't take advantage of the million-dollar view.

programme.title: Mega Decks: A Family Fun Deck With Plenty of Features
programme.desc: A 1970s-era sunroom and a small deck are taken down to create one space where a family can cook, eat, entertain, and stay warm. The finished project features a two-sided fireplace, water features, and custom privacy.

programme.title: Mega Decks: A Four-Season Party Deck for a Large Family
programme.desc: John and Bonnie have a very large family and a very small deck. And with their six adult children living nearby, they really want a place for the family to gather. So, they've decided to turn their old deck into the ultimate party deck.

programme.title: Mega Decks: A Luxury Deck with a Dog Staircase
programme.desc: Newlyweds Whitney and Joey have just purchased their dream home in an exclusive gated community, but the existing deck has some serious issues. It's old, rotting and doesn't fit their needs. Plus, their aging dog is having trouble using the stairs.

programme.title: Mega Decks: Colorado Contemporary Deck
programme.desc: The crew has a $350,000 budget for a contemporary-style mega deck with a curved hand-cut stone fireplace, a pergola and a pizza oven. Building outdoors in winter subjects the crew to dangerous icy conditions and an oversight threatens the pergola build.

programme.title: Mega Decks: Three Decks, Three Pergolas, a Swim Spa, and a Water Feature
programme.desc: Brad and Ronnie have gorgeous mountain views and five acres of land, but their patio and decks are faded and cracked. They have four separate outdoor spaces and the Mega Crew is challenged to update all of them.

programme.title: Mega Decks: A Deck Fit For Fun
programme.desc: A couple's home in Parker, Colorado, needs a major deck overhaul to take advantage of the incredible views of the Rocky Mountains and downtown Denver. A huge budget allows the Mega Decks crew to design deck like no other.

programme.title: Mega Decks: A Mountain Retreat with a Rocky View
programme.desc: The team contends with nearly impassable roads, mountain weather and a build site on top a granite boulder.

programme.title: Mega Decks: Log Cabin Luxe
programme.desc: To help this tiny deck achieve mega status, massive 40-foot-long Western red cedar logs are trucked in from Canada. The finished product has a three-tier pizza oven, a six-foot gas fire rail, and extra tall glass panels.

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation: Renovation in South Florida
programme.desc: A couple looks for a beach home to renovate on a budget in South Florida.

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation: Supersizing in St. Petersburg, Florida
programme.desc: A newly engaged couple loves their Florida lifestyle and desires to be on the water in St. Petersburg, which means they must take on a renovation project.

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation: Back to the Beach on the Jersey Shore
programme.desc: A couple looks for a beach house in Ventnor City, New Jersey, where they can make memories with their daughter on the Jersey Shore.

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation: Poolside Paradise in Cocoa Beach, Florida
programme.desc: Candice and Brian are moving from Denver, Colo. to Cocoa Beach, Fla., and have enlisted broker Andy Barclay to help them find a home on the water with a pool and a large outdoor space for their two daughters.

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation: Getting Set in Narragansett, Rhode Island
programme.desc: After renting vacation homes in Narragansett, Rhode Island, for many years, a couple finally buys a place of their own that needs a lot of work. Luckily he is a general contractor and ready to take on the challenge.

programme.title: Man v. Food: Hall of Fame: Best BBQ
programme.desc: Casey's quest for America's best bites has taken him from coast to coast, and it's about time we started celebrating the cream of the crop. Fire up the grill, because this time we’re inducting the Best BBQ in the history of Man V. Food.

programme.title: Man v. Food: Hall of Fame: Absolute Cheesiest
programme.desc: Casey’s criss-crossed the country chowing down on the best eats American has to offer, and its time to honour the best of the best by inducting the top five absolute cheesiest dishes in Man vs. Food history into our Hall of Fame.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Hawaii Fried Dough
programme.desc: Noah Cappe arrives in the Aloha State for the 'Iolani Fair. This school fundraiser fair in Honolulu features Hawaiian-style fried dough in the Malasada Ice Cream Sandwich and Sweet Roll Sliders.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Steak Out
programme.desc: Noah Cappe is in carnivore mode for the San Angelo Stock Show and Rodeo in Texas.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Toastbusters
programme.desc: Noah Cappe hits the Hunt County Fair in Greenville, Texas, and makes short work of the Smoke Bomb and the French toast crunch burger. But it's the blue bacon cheesecake that really knocks him out.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Deep-Fried Inferno
programme.desc: Where there's smoke, there's fire, and Noah Cappe is ready to have his taste buds fired up at the Spencerport Volunteer Firemen's Association Carnival in New York. From the Flaming Weck to the Five Alarm Fries, there's plenty of sizzle.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Grillers in the Mist
programme.desc: Noah Cappe heads to New York for the Long Island Fun Fest and some Empire State eats. He can't resist a pizza covered in gooey penne alla vodka or a fancy Hamptons Dog, and he also tries the ultimate Italian-American combo, a Cannoli Calzone.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: My Fair Latte
programme.desc: Noah Cappe starts off in Chippewa Falls, Wis., at the Northern Wisconsin State Fair. The Wisconsin woods are the inspiration for the loaded Campfire Burger, and the state's famous cheese curds make an appearance in the Hot Chick.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Pretzella Queen of the Dessert
programme.desc: Host Noah Cappe enjoys German inspired carnival treats at Christkindlmarket Milwaukee. First Noah digs into the Austrian Alpine Daug which packs a cheese-stuffed bratwurst into a hearty roll topped with sauerkraut and potato salad.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Camp Pop
programme.desc: Noah Cappe heads to northern Indiana, where the Hoosiers in Valparaiso are doing things right at the Porter County Fair. The Super Ramen Dog combines two delicious foods Noah didn't know belonged together, the Elephant Ear Taco.

programme.title: The Best Thing I Ever Ate: New Deli Done Right
programme.desc: America's best delicatessens are serving everything from perfected tried-and-true classics to inventive new spins on old favourites. Wolfgang Puck shares the best hot smoked salmon in the country.

programme.title: The Best Thing I Ever Ate: Special Occasion
programme.desc: Nothing makes a special occasion more memorable than having the best food! We're counting down the best meals for any special occasion, starting with Beau MacMillan's unforgettable gnocchi in San Diego.

programme.title: The Best Thing I Ever Ate: Genuine Legends

programme.title: The Best Thing I Ever Ate: Yummy in the Middle

programme.desc: Hop Pop drops Sprig and Polly off at an obstacle-filled mountain trail designed to help them get along. Sasha and Grime must reconcile their differences to escape Newtopia's most skilled warrior, General Yunnan.

programme.title: Amphibia: Cursed!/ Fiddle Me This
programme.desc: After Anne helps Sprig break up with Maddie, strange things start happening to them. When a popular talent competition comes to Wartwood, Hop Pop goes overboard in training Sprig to win.

programme.title: DuckTales: The House of the Lucky Gander
programme.desc: Donald competes with his cousin Gladstone Gander to impress Louie at a mysterious resort in Macaw, while Scrooge tries to lure the kids away from the resort's endless distractions.

programme.title: Big Hero 6: The Series: Something Fluffy
programme.desc: When mysterious but adorable creatures called Mayoi suddenly appear in San Fransokyo, Hiro is convinced that they’re part of some nefarious plot by Liv Amara.

programme.title: Polaris: Player Select: Mountain Out of a Molehill
programme.desc: A compilation of top gaming videos from YouTube stars.

programme.title: Polaris: Player Select: The Best Thing Since Slice Bread
programme.desc: A compilation of top gaming videos from YouTube stars.

programme.title: Marvel's Ultimate Spider-Man vs. The Sinister 6: Hydra Attacks
programme.desc: SHIELD finds itself caught off-guard and it is left to Spiderman to single-handedly stop the assault.

programme.title: Marvel's Ultimate Spider-Man vs. The Sinister 6: Hydra Attacks
programme.desc: SHIELD finds itself caught off-guard and it is left to Spiderman to single-handedly stop the assault.

programme.title: Marvel's Ultimate Spider-Man vs. The Sinister 6: Iron Vulture
programme.desc: Spider-Man must stop Doc Ock from eliminating Norman Osborne.

programme.title: Marvel's Ultimate Spider-Man vs. The Sinister 6: Lizards
programme.desc: Vulture's revelation that Doc Ock has a spy inside S.H.I.E.L.D. Academy leads the Web Warriors on a hunt to find the mole.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: The Stanchurian Candidate
programme.desc: When Grunkle Stan decides to run for mayor, Dipper and Mabel have their work cut out for them trying to turn their gaffe-prone uncle into the perfect candidate.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Roadside Attraction
programme.desc: Grunkle Stan takes the kids with him on a road-trip to sabotage all the other tourist traps in Oregon.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Elevator Action / Bad Influencer
programme.desc: Gloria gets stuck on an elevator with the Greens. Remy falls under the sway of an internet influencer.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Coffee Quest/ Phoenix Rises
programme.desc: When Big City faces a coffee bean shortage, Cricket and Gloria’s boss, Ms. Cho, challenges them to retrieve one of a few remaining sacks of coffee; When the Greens' dog, Phoenix, runs away from home, the family sets out to find her.

programme.title: Amphibia: Scavenger Hunt/ The Plantars Check In
programme.desc: Anne, Sprig and Marcy receive a mysterious message from the king of Newtopia that sends them on a puzzle-solving mission. Anne finally meets with King Andrias and tries to enlist his help in getting back home.

programme.title: Amphibia: Lost in Newtopia/ Sprig Gets Schooled
programme.desc: Anne and Polly vow to experience the city like locals but end up on a wild ride through the streets of Newtopia. Sprig is offered a spot at Newtopia University.

programme.title: Big Hero 6: The Series: Aunt Cass Goes Out
programme.desc: After Aunt Cass nearly figures out Hiro’s secret life as a member of Big Hero 6, Hiro plays matchmaker to find Aunt Cass a boyfriend to distract her.

programme.title: Big Hero 6: The Series: Muira-Horror!
programme.desc: Hiro and Go Go accompany Krei to the woods to deal with Nedd Ludd, however they run into the monster of legend, the Hibagon, along the way.

programme.title: Walk the Prank: Grandma's Here to Stay
programme.desc: Dusty is stressed when his Grandma moves into his house and is now sharing a room with him.

programme.title: Walk the Prank: So You Think You Can Middle School Dance
programme.desc: It's Herman's first school dance and the gang tries to teach him the ropes, but Herman ends up teaching them instead. Meanwhile, Uncle Will's mom is famous for her gigantic turkeys. Will invites a guest over a feast he won't soon forget.

programme.title: Walk the Prank: Blind Date
programme.desc: The gang finds out that Uncle Will has, unknowingly, been set up on a blind date with Bailey's mom. Meanwhile, a target is hired to be on-air talent for a home shopping network owned by Chance's dad.

programme.title: Walk the Prank: Teachers Break Their Promises
programme.desc: When the students learn their much-anticipated class trip has been cancelled due to budgetary reasons, they devise a plan to fight back. Meanwhile, Uncle Will, Dusty, Chance, and Herman turn the mall upside down to prank a security guard.

programme.title: Walk the Prank: Float Like a Butterfly, Sting Like a Flea
programme.desc: Herman accidentally draws the ire of a bully at school and prepares for the worst.

programme.title: Walk the Prank: Death of a Lima Bean
programme.desc: Herman is devastated when he accidentally kills the lima bean he had been cultivating as a class project and begins to worry about his own safety.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Headhunters
programme.desc: When someone tampers with Mabel's life-size wax statue, she and Dipper set out to find the culprit. Meanwhile, Grunkle Stan mourns over the loss of his wax doppelganger.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: The Legend of the Gobblewonker
programme.desc: The twins embark on a mission to prove that a legendary sea monster exists at the bottom of the local lake. Soos helps them on a boating mission to prove it exists.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Breaking News / Cyberbullies
programme.desc: When Bill grows a huge watermelon, Cricket invites the local news to see the mega melon. Cricket stands up to the cyberbullying of three online gamers known as the Cyber Knights.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Barry Cuda/ Suite Retreat
programme.desc: Cricket uses his first tip from working at Big Coffee to buy a singing toy fish. Gramma kicks the family out of the house for the day.

programme.title: Amphibia: Swamp and Sensibility/ Wax Museum
programme.desc: When Anne discovers an old friend who has been living a double life, she becomes fixated on helping him be his true self. The family visits a mysterious roadside oddities museum where every treasure comes with a price.

programme.title: Amphibia: The Ballad of Hopediah Plantar/ Anne Hunter
programme.desc: Hop Pop's efforts to be hailed as a hero amongst some small-town folks backfires when he stands up to the wrong family. Then, when the family is in danger, Anne must put her self-consciousness aside in order to learn the true way of the hunter.

programme.title: Kick Buttowski Suburban Daredevil: Runaway Recital/ Trike X-5
programme.desc: Kick's dad forces him to learn the piano, but Kick decides to turn it into a stunt that spans the whole town. Kick's sister steals his first stunt bike and turns it into a girly bike.

programme.title: Kick Buttowski Suburban Daredevil: Sister Pact/ Shh!
programme.desc: Kick makes a promise to Brianna to help her get in a prestigious girls club.

programme.title: Kick Buttowski Suburban Daredevil: Knocked Out/ Not Without My Cereal
programme.desc: Gunther gets in the way of Kick's stunt career/ Kick must look after his sister.

programme.title: Kick Buttowski Suburban Daredevil: Say Cheese/ Pinch Sitter
programme.desc: Kick and his siblings must overcome their differences to replace a picture they broke.

programme.title: Kick Buttowski Suburban Daredevil: You've Been Brad'd/ Sleepover
programme.desc: After Kick discovers that he is the sole target of Brad's new Internet prank show, he must find a way to avoid his brother and complete an impossible stunt.

programme.title: Kick Buttowski Suburban Daredevil: Only the Loan-ly/ Roll Reversal
programme.desc: Kick borrows money from Brianna and faces her wrath when he can't pay it back.

programme.title: Kick Buttowski Suburban Daredevil: Dead Man's Drop/ Stumped
programme.desc: Kick's newest and terrifying stunt is conquering Dead Man's Drop but Brad interrupts.

programme.title: Kick Buttowski Suburban Daredevil: Kicked Out/ Kick the Habit
programme.desc: When Kick's new hangout is taken over by Brad and his friends, Kick does everything in his power to keep the newfound space as his own.

programme.title: Kick Buttowski Suburban Daredevil: Snowpolcalypse!/ According to Chimp
programme.desc: Kick causes the school bus to get trapped during a blizzard. Later, Kick brings home a lost chimp named Dr. Awesome and makes it his new pet. Kick soon realizes that the apparently cute little primate becomes destructive without chocolate bananas.

programme.title: Kick Buttowski Suburban Daredevil: Those Who Camp, Do/ Dog Gone
programme.desc: Brad decides to tag along when Kick vows to spend a night in the untamed wilderness and make it out alive. Later, Kick must track down a ferocious devil dog before Mrs. Chicarelly gets home and gets him grounded for life.

programme.title: Kick Buttowski Suburban Daredevil: Mellowbrook Drift/ Gift of Wacky
programme.desc: Kick challenges the physics nerd bully to a winner-takes-all, gravity-defying race.

programme.title: Kick Buttowski Suburban Daredevil: Frame Story/ And ... Action!
programme.desc: The principal accuses Kick of a crime he didn't commit, threatening to expel him from school. When Kick meets his female counterpart in a popular TV actress, he helps her discover her inner daredevil.

programme.title: Beyblade Burst Rise: Battle in the Skies!
programme.desc: Dante challenges Pheng to a rematch. After the battle, the gang heads to Battle Island to compete in a Blading circuit.

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit: Persona

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit: Transitions

programme.title: How It Really Happened: Boston Marathon: The Manhunt (Part 2)
programme.desc: With the Boston Bombing suspects identified as domestic terrorist brothers, the manhunt begins, a terrifying crime spree ensues, and new information sheds light on the heinous crime.

programme.title: Airplane Repo: The Blonde Bomber

programme.title: 100 Days Wild: Under House Arrest

programme.title: 100 Days Wild: Zombie Fish Apocalypse

programme.title: Deadliest Catch: Bering Sea Crash

programme.title: Deadliest Catch: Gut Instinct

programme.title: Deadliest Catch: Bering Sea Wrecking Ball
programme.desc: Stuffed pots come at a price as Jake scrambles to repair a slack tank before it kills his crab or capsizes the Saga. Josh and Casey's rudder fails in heavy traffic and Wild Bill's new greenhorn makes an easy target for an out-of-control picking hook.

programme.title: Deadliest Catch: Danger Close
programme.desc: Josh and Casey race to haul pots as engine failure threatens their delivery window. As seawater destroys his hydraulic motors, Junior attempts a risky high seas delivery of three new motors. Harley and his crew bang heads over his fishing strategy.

programme.title: Deadliest Catch: Like Father Like Daughter
programme.desc: Sig and Mandy conspire to get Jake to do their prospecting, but the former deckhand seizes the moment to fill his own tanks. The Wizard crew must break through ice to rush Keith to a doctor. Junior fears the worst as a deckhand falls from the stack.

programme.title: Say Yes to the Dress: Daddy-Daughter Dilemma
programme.desc: Caitlin has her fitting now that her father is healthy enough to attend.

programme.title: Say Yes to the Dress: A Dress like None the Rest

programme.title: Say Yes to the Dress: Dazed and Gown-fused
programme.desc: Bride Nicole comes in with a very specific style of dress in mind, but discovers it may not suit her. Indecisive Katy has already tried on 50 gowns elsewhere and still doesn't know what she wants. Cy can't choose between two perfect dresses.

programme.desc: Eccentric Marlene wants a feathered or black ball gown, but her parents prefer something traditional. Jessica loves every dress she tries on, but her family likes none. Angelina is head-over-heels for a bohemian gown, but no one in her group is on board.

programme.title: Say Yes to the Dress: Breaking the Bank

programme.title: The Zoo: San Diego: Welcome to Our World
programme.desc: A baby klipspringer is born on one of the coldest nights of the season and is miraculously nursed back to health. The California condor team at the Safari Park goes to unbelievable lengths to save this highly endangered species.

programme.title: The Zoo: San Diego: Yeti and the Cubs
programme.desc: There will be no peace in the world for the hamadryas baboons until Impo finds his mate. Luckily, the San Diego Zoo keepers have a plan. Two young cheetah cubs are training to become animal ambassadors with the help of their experienced canine guide.

programme.title: The Zoo: San Diego: New Day for an Old Tortoise
programme.desc: Hunter the koala is ready for a mate. Jaws the Galápagos tortoise isn't feeling well and the zoo staff works to bring him out of his shell. The Safari Park has not one but two baby giraffe calves that need to be hand-reared simultaneously.

programme.title: The Zoo: Higher Purpose
programme.desc: Two adorable tiger cubs have outgrown their keepers and need to move to the adult exhibit. A silverback gorilla receives pioneering treatment to curb his worsening eyesight. And a passionate keeper plays matchmaker to a peculiar pair of birds.

programme.title: The Zoo: Moving Day
programme.desc: The Zoo's huge male sea lion is in rut, and staff need to move the younger males before he comes territorial. The veterinary team come up with an ingenious solution to repair Dinky the Flamingo's broken ankle. And feeding a rock python proves challenging.

programme.title: The Zoo: Handle With Care
programme.desc: A tomistoma, a freshwater crocodilian, stops laying eggs and a team of keepers scrambles to treat her. The veterinary team has to rescue a baby antelope that isn't getting the care it needs from its mother. And Mert the Goose takes a field trip.

programme.title: The Zoo: A Star Is Born
programme.desc: The birth of a little blue penguin becomes causes a sensation at the Bronx Zoo. The veterinary team scrambles to figure out what is wrong with Snowball, a snow leopard. And the exhibits team designs an exhibit for the newly arrived blue iguana.

programme.title: The Zoo: Birds and the Bees
programme.desc: Leo, a snow leopard rescued from Pakistan, is having trouble eating because of a broken canine. The bird department works feverishly to restore a beehive for the bee-eater birds. Ambassador Center staff work with an adorable fennec fox named Charlie.

programme.title: The Zoo: Walk on the Wild Side
programme.desc: The future of the American bison depends on a high-stakes race across the country. The reptile staff have their hands full with a diamond rattlesnake that needs to be medicated orally. A pair of cheetahs is trained to walk the grounds of the zoo.

programme.title: The Zoo: Love Shack
programme.desc: To help love bloom between two kiwis, zoo staff build an elaborate hut into which they introduce a pair of potential mates. When a king cobra arrives at the zoo, keepers have their hands full training him to move from the exhibit to a holding pen.

programme.title: Extinct or Alive: The Lost Shark
programme.desc: Forrest Galante heads to the Maldives to find a supposedly extinct shark. Unidentified sharks in the area have led many to believe it's alive. He will dive into an untamed wilderness to answer once and for all is the Pondicherry extinct or alive.

programme.title: 10 Things You Don't Know About: American Revolution
programme.desc: Hear tales of overlooked heroes, astonishing innovations and heartbreaking tragedy that happened during the American Revolution.

programme.title: 10 Things You Don't Know About: Founding Fathers
programme.desc: Henry Rollins takes your preconceived notions of these founding fathers and turn them on their ear. There are unbelievable innovations, bitter rivalries, spy games and even bankrupt presidents.

programme.title: 10 Things You Don't Know About: The White House
programme.desc: Located on 1600 Pennsylvania Avenue, the White House is one of the most recognizable homes in the world. Henry Rollins takes a look at the shape of the Oval Office and uncovers why a brothel once operated on White House grounds.

programme.title: 10 Things You Don't Know About: The Flag
programme.desc: The American flag is one of the most familiar symbols in the world, but it has more stories than you've heard. From its first design to the original pledge, from the Civil War to the Moon, the American flag has taken a journey unlike any other.

programme.title: Secret Access: Superpower
programme.desc: An all-access pass to America's military might, including weapons and technology. America's most remarkable munitions of past and present.

programme.title: Secrets of the Founding Fathers

programme.title: America's Book of Secrets: The Monuments
programme.desc: Go behind the walls of the Washington Monument to reveal a series of secret symbols and codes, one which was put there by one of the world's most mysterious societies.

programme.desc: A teenager with a penchant for detective stories stumbles upon a real-life mystery.

programme.desc: A New York lawyer tries to track down a kidnapped actress.

programme.title: Movie: Eyes in the Night
programme.desc: A blind detective tries to help a friend, but gets mixed up with enemy agents and murder.

programme.title: Movie: The Hidden Eye

programme.title: Movie: Ratatouille
programme.desc: An ambitious rat with a flair for cooking battles an eccentric chef in a Paris restaurant.

programme.title: Raven's Home: Level Up
programme.desc: Booker, Nia and Tess team up to win the end of the year 8th-grade treasure hunt in hopes they can become Carver Middle school legends. Meanwhile, Raven and Chelsea land a segment to promote their business on Chris Spring-Lake's new reality makeover show.

programme.title: Raven's Home: Smoky Flow
programme.desc: The kids form a band called the Chi-Lectives and film a video in Tess’ apartment. When things get out of hand with a smoke machine, Raven discovers they were home alone.

programme.title: Raven's Home: Twister, Sister
programme.desc: When Booker challenges Nia's efforts as an activist, she follows her dad to where he's reporting on a storm to prove to herself and her brother that she is indeed an activist and not a "positivist."

programme.title: Raven's Home: Friend-Ship
programme.desc: Raven and the kids plan a road trip to visit Chelsea at her cruise ship and encounter several obstacles along the way.

programme.title: Raven's Home: Lost at Chel-Sea
programme.desc: The kids accidentally get stuck on a cruise ship alone, while Raven and Chelsea desperately try to catch up with them via Jet Ski.

programme.title: Jessie: Basket Case
programme.desc: Eager to play in Luke's basketball game, Ravi unexpectedly runs into LA Clippers star Chris Paul, who he enlists to coach him. Meanwhile, Jessie learns that Hudson is her new classmate and is unhappily forced to partner with him for an assignment.

programme.title: Jessie: Someone Has Tou-pay
programme.desc: Bertram bought a new toupee for his band's big performance, but when it goes missing, Jessie interrogates each of the kids in hopes of finding it.

programme.title: Jessie: Bye Bye Bertie
programme.desc: Bertram has had enough, so decides to quit and to go work for Mrs. Chesterfield. Meanwhile, the Ross family hires a new butler, Roger, who takes his job very seriously.

programme.title: Sydney to the Max: Bummer Rental
programme.desc: It's Fourth of July weekend, and Max surprises Sydney, Olive and Sophia by renting the lake cabin where he spent his best summer as a kid.

programme.title: Sydney to the Max: My Best Friends' Ending
programme.desc: Sydney hosts a slumber party with all of her friends, but the night takes a turn when Emmy and Sophia have a huge disagreement, reminiscent of when a pair of expensive sneakers came between Max and Leo in the '90s.

programme.title: Jessie: Cattle Calls and Scary Walls
programme.desc: Jessie auditions for a role in a television series and is convinced that she can talk her way into getting the part. However, it's Zuri who impresses the director. Emma, who Jessie left in charge at home, joins Ravi and Luke to look for rats in the walls.

programme.title: Jessie: Green-Eyed Monsters
programme.desc: Jessie joins a comedy improv class, and Tony goes to embarrassing lengths to prove his love for Jessie. Meanwhile, Ravi recruits Zuri to help him babysit Mrs. Kipling's baby lizards.

programme.title: Bunk'd: Close Encounters of the Camp Kind
programme.desc: After the campers think they see a UFO land in Lake Kikiwaka, a new boy shows up and Jorge and Tiffany think he's an alien. Meanwhile, Emma and Ravi get evaluated by the camp counselors.

programme.title: Raven's Home: Slammed
programme.desc: When Booker becomes responsible for choosing who will represent the class at the district poetry slam, Nia thinks she has a guaranteed spot on the team. Meanwhile, Raven and Chelsea start a DIY renovation on their new business space.

programme.title: Raven's Home: On Edge
programme.desc: When Mr. Arthur tells Booker that he isn't ready to go from sweeping floors to cutting hair at the barbershop, Booker opens his own mobile barbershop in the boys' school bathroom.

programme.title: Liv and Maddie: Co-Star-A-Rooney
programme.desc: Casting is underway for Liv's co-star in 'Voltage' and some familiar faces show up for the auditions. Meanwhile, Karen arranges a 'cleansing ceremony' for Maddie to help her let go of her feelings for Diggie.

programme.title: Liv and Maddie: Meatball-A-Rooney
programme.desc: Willow helps Maddie send in a video application for a basketball scholarship. When Willow gets notified that she has received a scholarship and Maddie hasn't heard yet, Maddie gets jealous and blows up at Willow.

programme.title: Austin & Ally: Club Owners & Quinceaneras
programme.desc: When Austin hears that a big-time club owner will be at Trish's Quinceañera, the gang plans a stand-out performance to ensure Austin gets noticed. Trish invites Dallas to the party, and Ally is nervous about dancing with him.

programme.title: Austin & Ally: Songwriting & Starfish
programme.desc: The gang tries to help Austin get ready for the Hot Summer Jam Contest on the beach, where he could win a chance to have his song played on the radio. Meanwhile, Ally tries to put her previous unpleasant beach-going experiences behind her.

programme.title: Bunk'd: Waka, Waka, Waka!
programme.desc: Ravi and Tiffany get video proof that the Kikiwaka exists. Meanwhile, Emma and Lou try to outsell Hazel at the Kikiwaka Day Festival.

programme.title: Bunk'd: Boo Boos and Birthdays
programme.desc: Emma and Zuri butt heads when trying to plan a surprise birthday party for Tiffany. Meanwhile, in order to get out of a volleyball tournament, Ravi and Jorge fake injuries.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Desserted/ The Gifted
programme.desc: Cricket treats his family to dinner, but Bill is suspicious about how he plans to pay for it. Tilly and Cricket battle to see if Bill lies about liking their Father's Day gifts.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Mixed-Up Adventures: Donald's Fast Food 500/ Mini-Helpers
programme.desc: Mickey and his friends plan to surprise Donald with a waffle breakfast for his birthday. Minnie's nieces, Millie and Melody, visit the Happy Helpers office for the day.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Mixed-Up Adventures: Goofy's Hot Dog Harvest/ Puppy Birthday to You
programme.desc: Mickey and friends go on adventures around Hot Dog Hills.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: Cousin Cody/ Hissy's Lost To
programme.desc: The pugs help their nervous puppy cousin, Cody, feel comfortable in a new place. When Hissy’s favorite toy goes missing, the pups go on a mission to find it for their kitty sister.

programme.desc: When Bob's finishing touch for Puppy Playcare breaks, Bingo and Rolly make it their mission to find a replacement before Playcare opens! The Pugs help Hissy nap while at Playcare!

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: Pups in the Apple / Won't You Be My Puppy

programme.desc: When Pip and Freddy have to deliver a kitten, they think it's a mistake since there are no cats at T.O.T.S. The Junior Fliers must deliver a baby chameleon that keeps disappearing!

programme.title: T.O.T.S.: Stripe Out/ A Splashy Delivery
programme.desc: Pip and Freddy try to find out what mysterious striped animal they are delivering. While delivering a baby octopus, Pip and Freddy accidentally unplug the crate.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Shorts: Flushed!
programme.desc: Mickey Mouse finds himself in silly situations around the world.

programme.title: Miraculous: Tales of Ladybug and Cat Noir: Frightningale
programme.desc: A young American singer gets akumatized into Frightningale. Armed with a magic mike, she wants to turn Paris into a giant musical. Ladybug and Cat Noir will have to keep the rhythm if they want to stop he.

programme.title: Miraculous: Tales of Ladybug and Cat Noir: Gorizilla
programme.desc: Adrien's bodyguard gets akumatized into Gorizalla. Endowed with extraordinary strength and an acute sense of smell, he tracks Adrien all through Paris. Ladybug and Cat Noir can't monkey around if they want to stop him.

programme.title: Miraculous: Tales of Ladybug and Cat Noir: Robostus
programme.desc: A small, sensitive robot named Markov gets akumatized into Robostus, a villain who can bring inanimate objects to life.

programme.title: Miraculous: Tales of Ladybug and Cat Noir: Sapotis
programme.desc: Alya's little sisters get akumatized into small, out-of-control monsters named Sapotis who wreak havoc in Paris. Epic babysitting session ahead for Ladybug and Cat Noir.

programme.title: Amphibia: The Ballad of Hopediah Plantar/ Anne Hunter
programme.desc: Hop Pop's efforts to be hailed as a hero amongst some small-town folks backfires when he stands up to the wrong family. Then, when the family is in danger, Anne must put her self-consciousness aside in order to learn the true way of the hunter.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Shark Objects / Dream Weaver
programme.desc: When Greens try to spend an eventful day at the beach, Cricket tries to prank the beach-goers; Cricket discovers he can influence the family's dreams to disastrous real-life results.

programme.title: Bunk'd: Dance in My Pants
programme.desc: Camp Kikiwaka hosts its annual dance which has Tiffany and Zuri hopeful that they'll dance with a cute boy. Emma learns that Lou and Xander have a history.

programme.title: Raven's Home: Dream Moms
programme.desc: When Booker and Levi get into some trouble backstage at a concert, Raven and Chelsea try to seize the moment to perform with their all-time favorite group La Dee Da.

programme.title: Raven's Home: Because
programme.desc: Raven opens up about her own visions to the kids, sharing a special moment with Booker. The two team up to try and locate the missing money. Nia feels left out and fakes having a vision.

programme.title: Bunk'd: Zuri Had a Little Lamb
programme.desc: Lou assigns Zuri and Emma to barn duty. Meanwhile, the boys discover that Gladys has been hiding a secret in the woods.

programme.title: Bunk'd: Zuri Weasels Out

programme.title: Miraculous: Tales of Ladybug and Cat Noir: Silencer

programme.title: NCIS: Charade
programme.desc: After Tony's identity is stolen by multiple perpetrators, the fake Special Agent DiNozzos begin blackmailing Senators.

programme.title: NCIS: Return to Sender
programme.desc: Gibbs and Senior FBI Agent T.C. Fornell unite after two British prisoners, including a former spy, escape and arrive stateside via a shipping container. Also, McGee is busy apartment hunting and determined to find out how DiNozzo bought his lavish place.

programme.title: NCIS: Decompressed
programme.desc: When a deep sea diver is murdered on the job, his body and suspected coworkers must remain in a high-tech decompression chamber for four days, leaving the team to complete an unorthodox investigation from outside the tank.

programme.title: NCIS: React
programme.desc: When Secretary of the Navy Sarah Porter learns that her daughter has been kidnapped, the NCIS team partners with the FBI to track the case, determine a motive and bring her home. Also, McGee's childhood friend is in town to assist the team with training.

programme.title: NCIS: Loose Cannons
programme.desc: Dr. Cyril Taft experiences the rush of an NCIS case when he is asked to perform surgery on Gibbs' suspect and finds key evidence. Also, Dinozzo pays Jeanne Benoit a visit as he chases a lead involving doctors in Sudan.

programme.title: NCIS: After Hours
programme.desc: The NCIS agents' personal plans are interrupted when they each catch errors in a seemingly cut-and-dry case. Also, McGee and Delilah argue over the importance of not discussing work during dinner, while DiNozzo bores his date with shop talk.

programme.title: NCIS: Scope
programme.desc: The NCIS team re-examines an ambush on a group of Special Ops snipers in Iraq after an American couple is attacked in the same area six months later. When Gibbs needs the account of the lone survivor, he tries to connect with him at Walter Reed Medical.

programme.title: NCIS: Reasonable Doubts
programme.desc: The NCIS team must ascertain who is lying, the wife or the mistress, after the women accuse each other of murdering a Navy Public Affairs officer. Also, Anthony DiNozzo Sr. helps a homeless woman who believes he is her father.

programme.title: NCIS: Charade
programme.desc: After Tony's identity is stolen by multiple perpetrators, the fake Special Agent DiNozzos begin blackmailing Senators.

programme.title: NCIS: Return to Sender
programme.desc: Gibbs and Senior FBI Agent T.C. Fornell unite after two British prisoners, including a former spy, escape and arrive stateside via a shipping container. Also, McGee is busy apartment hunting and determined to find out how DiNozzo bought his lavish place.

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent: Best Defense
programme.desc: The detectives suspect the wife of a lawyer when he kills a hit man in his house.

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent: Chinoiserie
programme.desc: When a Chinese immigrant is shot dead, the detectives think it was someone she knew.

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent: Malignant
programme.desc: The murders of two deliverymen lead the detectives to a pharmaceutical drug supplier.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: Knocked the Family Right Out
programme.desc: After a luxury condo is robbed, the team is shocked to learn that not only was the family fast asleep when the crime occurred, but their teenaged daughter was violated during the invasion as well.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: Looking Out for Stateville
programme.desc: Voight convinces Deputy Crowley to bring in his former cellmate, Eddie, as a CI for the unit in order to help bring down a big-time cocaine supplier and his local dealers.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: Hit Me
programme.desc: Lindsay goes undercover in an effort to track down a rogue police officer targeting women leaving a local casino.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: The Song of Gregory William Yates
programme.desc: Gregory Yates escapes from prison and the team learns he intends to flee to New York. The SVU team informs Intelligence that he has been gathering information on Lindsay.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: A Night Owl
programme.desc: While working at a sobriety checkpoint, Burgess finds a duffle bag full of heroin in the truck of a respected college professor who claims to know nothing about how the drugs ended up there.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: The Cases That Need to Be Solved
programme.desc: When a young boy is executed, Intelligence turns to the boy's parents for information. Upon further investigation, they discover the boy's father has a criminal background and was part of a local gang.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: Kasual With a K
programme.desc: While responding to a call of shots fired, Voight, Antonio and Halstead come across a secret battered women's shelter and discover the owner has been wounded.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: If We Were Normal
programme.desc: When a call comes in about a woman stabbing a man then fleeing the scene, Burgess and Lindsay go investigate and find a young woman with the weapon in her hand.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: In a Duffle Bag
programme.desc: Voight responds to a call about a suspicious duffle bag, and uncovers a frigid newborn baby. Determined to find the culprit who abandoned the infant, Intelligence receives a promising lead based on information provided by Chicago Med.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: Justice
programme.desc: After a hooded man opens fire on their patrol car, Roman is left wounded and Burgess takes off after the shooter. No gun is found at the crime scene and the victim is a 17-year-old honour student who will likely never walk again.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: Knocked the Family Right Out
programme.desc: After a luxury condo is robbed, the team is shocked to learn that not only was the family fast asleep when the crime occurred, but their teenaged daughter was violated during the invasion as well.

programme.title: Movie: 300
programme.desc: When a massive army sweeps into his land, Sparta's King Leonidas assembles a small but dedicated army to go to battle against the Persians.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Treehouse of Horror XXIII
programme.desc: A Mayan Homer and Marge are witnesses to the prediction that 2012 will be the end of the world, the Springfield Subatomic Supercollider creates a black hole that terrorizes the city and Bart travels back in time to 1974.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Adventures in Baby Getting
programme.desc: When Marge's car falls into a sink hole, she buys the perfect replacement. However, she grows to hate it since, as she tells a shocked Homer, it would be too small if she wanted to have another baby.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Gone Abie Gone
programme.desc: When Grampa escapes from his retirement home, Homer and Marge frantically try to track him down by following clues that reveal secrets in Grampa's past.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Penny Wiseguys
programme.desc: Homer is shocked to discover that his bowling teammate is an accountant for Fat Tony.

programme.title: The Simpsons: A Tree Grows in Springfield
programme.desc: Lisa tries to cheer up a down-in-the-dumps Homer by winning him a 'MyPad' at the school auction. But he falls back into a funk when he breaks the magical tablet device.

programme.title: The Simpsons: The Day the Earth Stood Cool
programme.desc: Homer wants a younger, hipper image, so he starts hanging out with Terrence and Emily - his cool new neighbours from Portland, Oregon, but Marge finds their trendy parenting methods extreme.

programme.title: The Simpsons: To Cur With Love
programme.desc: When a fire at the retirement home forces Grampa to move home with the Simpsons, Homer throws out his back on moving day and decides to stay home to recover.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Homer Goes to Prep School
programme.desc: Homer joins the 'Springfield Preppers,' an off-the-grid survivalist group whose leader has set up a top-secret retreat outside of town to prepare for the end of the world.

programme.title: The Simpsons: The Changing of the Guardian
programme.desc: After surviving a tornado, Marge and Homer seek out guardians for the kids, in case the worst should happen. They first turn to friends and family, including Homer's half-brother.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Love Is a Many Splintered Thing
programme.desc: Bart's heart strings are pulled once again when Mary Spuckler returns to Springfield. But his failure to pay her enough attention strains their relationship.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Gorgeous Grampa

programme.title: The Simpsons: Black-Eyed, Please

programme.title: It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia: The Gang Broke Dee

programme.desc: Tired of failing to be recognized for their years of service in the bar industry, the gang changes their ways to give the patrons what they think they want.

programme.desc: Dr. Pol hits the road to House Farms to handle an emergency calving, where a bone has cracked and fallen into the pelvis of the cow, blocking the calf’s way out.
